items.question: What does k-means minimize?
options: Within-cluster sum of squared Euclidean distances (WCSS)
items.answer: Within-cluster sum of squared Euclidean distances (WCSS)
items.explanation: k-means assigns points to minimize total squared Euclidean distance to centroids; centroids are updated as cluster means under that objective.

items.question: Which pair correctly describes Lloyd's algorithm?
options: Assign to nearest centroid, then update centroids to cluster means
Update centroids randomly, then delete smallest cluster
Merge closest clusters until k remain
Label noise points, then fit a density model
items.answer: Assign to nearest centroid, then update centroids to cluster means
items.explanation: Lloyd's loop alternates hard assignment and mean recomputation until assignments converge.

items.question: Why standardize features before k-means?
options: So large-scale features do not dominate Euclidean distance
To guarantee global optimum of WCSS
items.answer: So large-scale features do not dominate Euclidean distance
items.explanation: Unscaled mixed-unit data makes distance geometry meaningless; preprocessing defines what 'similar' means.

items.question: What happens to inertia as k increases toward n?
options: It decreases, reaching zero at k=n
items.answer: It decreases, reaching zero at k=n
items.explanation: Each extra cluster can only lower or maintain WCSS; at k=n every point is its own cluster with zero within-cluster error.
